expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains that target_sampling_rate sets the downsampled output rate of LFPV1
expectations.evidence: Says the field tells the LFP pipeline 'what sampling rate, in Hz, the output LFP should be written at after filtering and decimation'; describes the decimation step that produces the stored output.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Connects the rate to the Nyquist constraint for downstream band filters (e.g., ripple band 150–250 Hz needs > 500 Hz sampling)
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Nyquist — the band you want literally isn't there' walks through Nyquist explicitly for 500/400/300/250 Hz target rates and the 150–250 Hz ripple band.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names 1000 Hz as the canonical default and the consequence of going lower (aliasing in the downstream band)
expectations.evidence: States '1000 Hz is the safe, conventional choice', the default is 1000 Hz, and going lower causes aliasing back into the band.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not say the sample rate is arbitrary or that any value works (this would contradict the Nyquist constraint)
expectations.evidence: Explicit recommendation 'don't go below 1000 Hz for ripple work'; rate is constrained by Nyquist + filter design.
